analysis_programs.required_order: the source hashes above are committed and pushed before fresh P0 runs; the final analyzer must reject a mismatched preregistration hash

human_only_gates_not_satisfied: external cold ML and statistics reviews
human authorship and AAAI policy verification
phase: final P0 IID-LTT, attacker-portfolio, and natural-mixture confirmation
primary_claim: On fresh data, VERA evaluates a representation edit against a declared supported shift and five registered retrained attacker families; IID LTT is evaluated against the same edits, evidence, contracts, and shifts.

analysis_tiers.development: all completed seeds 45-172 may choose this protocol, but are never pooled with P0 confirmation
analysis_tiers.primary: independent P0 confirmation on seeds 173-236 at Gamma=1.25 with the expanded registered attacker portfolio
analysis_tiers.secondary: predeclared Gamma=1, 1.1, and 1.5 profiles, evidence budgets, and natural group-mixture diagnostics on the same fresh seeds

construction_receipt_schema.purpose: make the construction-only design decision independently replayable without revealing certification or external outcomes

contracts.confidence: Exact one-sided Clopper-Pearson bounds for binary class recalls and exact positive-upper/negative-lower Clopper-Pearson bounds for paired {-1,0,1} target harm.
contracts.envelope_multiplicity: The reported post-selection shift envelope uses simultaneous Bonferroni coverage over every registered candidate-contract pair.
contracts.leakage: For every preregistered attacker, leakage is one half of the robust class-conditional recall for source class 0 plus one half of the robust class-conditional recall for source class 1. It is therefore invariant to source-class prior shift and equals 0.5 for a constant binary attacker.
contracts.primary_multiplicity: Each candidate is an intersection-union test: every component contract must reject violation at alpha delta/12. A union bound over the 12 registered candidates controls selection of any unsafe candidate. No multiplicity penalty across component contracts is required for the intersection-union test; each balanced-leakage component splits its candidate alpha across two class recalls.
contracts.shift_model: Target contracts permit arbitrary changes in environment mixture weights and bound the within-environment deployment-to-certification density ratio by Gamma. Balanced leakage fixes source-class weights at one half and separately bounds each within-source-class density ratio by Gamma. The guarantee applies to any external law satisfying these support and density-ratio assumptions; benchmark external results test, but do not prove, assumption membership.
contracts.support_rule: A declared deployment environment absent from certification, or a missing source class required by balanced leakage, makes the contract non-identifiable and forces VERA to abstain. Camelyon17 center 2 is the preregistered unsupported case and its external balanced leakage is reported as NA.
contracts.target: Maximum environment-conditional mean paired increase in zero-one target loss versus the identity edit.

controlled_shift_protocol.certification_stream: independent with-replacement draws from the finite reference law under the locked evidence allocation
controlled_shift_protocol.claim_boundary: this validates the controlled finite-reference stress family, not membership of future people, images, comments, recordings, or hospitals
controlled_shift_protocol.final_evaluation: exact Q-weighted risks are primary labels; a separate 50,000-draw stream is a sampling diagnostic only
controlled_shift_protocol.membership_receipt: record the Q probability hash, selected construction-only cell, exact induced profiles, and machine-checked density-ratio membership
controlled_shift_protocol.profile: use the exact induced environment-conditional and source-conditional caps, never the global Gamma label alone
controlled_shift_protocol.scientific_object: each seed's finite empirical reference law over untouched official validation audit atoms
controlled_shift_protocol.shift_profile_family: bounded density-ratio reweightings with Gamma in {1, 1.1, 1.25, 1.5}
controlled_shift_protocol.stress_design_rule: On construction-only data, first select the edit with the largest construction target balanced accuracy (ties: candidate key). Among (environment, source, target) cells present in both construction and certification, select the lexicographically tied cell with the largest positive surplus: the maximum of mean paired target harm minus that dataset's target-harm threshold and, across the five registered attackers, mean source-correctness minus that dataset's balanced-leakage threshold. Certification, external, and held-out KNN outcomes cannot enter either selection.
controlled_shift_protocol.weight_rule: assign the selected supported cell density ratio Gamma and assign the unique nonnegative residual ratio to every other atom in that environment; all other environments retain ratio one

deployment_rules.always_deploy: Deploy the construction-selected fixed design edit without checking any certification contract or interval.
deployment_rules.common_evidence: All rules evaluate the same 12 candidate edits on the same simulated certification streams. Among eligible candidates, resolve selection by lowest sampled maximum registered-attacker balanced leakage, then lowest sampled maximum target harm, then ascending candidate key.
deployment_rules.exact_shift_oracle: For retention accounting only, select from candidates whose exact finite reference-law shifted target and registered-attacker risks meet all thresholds. It is not a deployable rule.
deployment_rules.iid_ltt: Deploy the common-evidence selected candidate only if its simultaneous candidate-wise exact IUT clears all target and five-attacker contracts at the IID profile (all profile coordinates equal one).
deployment_rules.validation_point_selection: Deploy the common-evidence selected candidate only if every sampled point target and registered-attacker leakage estimate is at or below its declared threshold; no confidence interval is used.
deployment_rules.vera_vector_envelope: Deploy the common-evidence selected candidate only if its simultaneous five-attacker vector envelope contains the exact declared target and source conditional profile induced by the construction-selected cell.

evidence_allocation.score_rule: use construction-only margins for the fixed design edit and allocate the remaining integer budget by squared Gamma-over-margin scores with deterministic largest-remainder rounding

exclusions_and_missingness.allowed_exclusion: only a mechanically corrupt or unverifiable receipt, reported by seed, dataset, and method with no replacement
exclusions_and_missingness.missing_run_policy: the matrix is incomplete until restored; missing runs may not be counted as abstentions, safe decisions, or violations

real_study.external_evaluation: After every representation, probe, attacker, candidate, and rule is fixed, evaluate the same target and balanced-leakage contracts on the untouched external split. External balanced leakage is NA when either source class is absent. Naive deployment into a preregistered unsupported environment is counted separately as procedurally unsupported, not relabeled as a measured contract violation.

required_order: commit and push this protocol with its SHA-256 sidecar
run only fresh seeds 173-236 into the P0 directories
seal two independent analyzers before reading scientific outcomes
report failed and successful endpoints without pooling prior seeds

headline_stress_family.source: deterministic design on seeds 5-12, frozen before seeds 13-44

heldout_attacker.name: knn_distance
heldout_attacker.usage: external stress evaluation only; never used by certification or selection

reported_outputs: portfolio-safe edits remaining safe under boosted-tree stress
registered attacker most predictive of held-out failure

Bios.source: frozen before the controlled-shift study; no fresh outcome may change this threshold

CivilComments-WILDS.source: frozen before the controlled-shift study; no fresh outcome may change this threshold

GaitPDB.source: frozen before the controlled-shift study; no fresh outcome may change this threshold

Waterbirds.source: frozen before the controlled-shift study; no fresh outcome may change this threshold

missingness.outcome_reporting: all predeclared primary and secondary results are reported regardless of success
missingness.policy: a missing or corrupt receipt fails the matrix and is restored with the same seed; no performance-based replacement or exclusion is allowed

natural_group_mixture_protocol.deployment_split: the untouched native external split supplied by each public benchmark
natural_group_mixture_protocol.estimand: observed target harm and portfolio leakage under its naturally occurring environment mixture, together with the validation-to-external mixture shift
natural_group_mixture_protocol.interpretation: the observed natural split is a realistic supported-mixture evaluation only to the degree that the stated conditional-stability assumption is credible; it is reported separately from the exact guarantee
natural_group_mixture_protocol.purpose: practical relevance diagnostic distinct from the exact controlled study
required_reporting: IID LTT versus VERA deployment, safety, and retention
one accepted, one abstained, and one unsupported-support case
natural_group_mixture_protocol.supported_shift_assumption: within-environment conditional laws are stable and only environment mixture weights change

post_failure_reporting.failed_followup_wording: independent follow-up failed; no secondary or exploratory result may replace it
post_failure_reporting.successful_followup_wording: independent follow-up passed after increasing the locked evidence budget; the first controlled-shift primary remains a mixed result

real_study.preprocessing: Standardize from eraser-train only and fit randomized PCA from eraser-train only.

heldout_knn_stress.success: report all deployments and a one-sided 95% interval; do not describe zero events as universal erasure

iid_ltt_exposure.estimand: fraction of IID LTT deployments that satisfy the IID certificate but violate the exact declared shifted contract
iid_ltt_exposure.success: at least 20% IID LTT shifted violations in at least one of the four predeclared Gamma profiles, reported with a one-sided interval

paired_comparison.test: two-sided exact sign test for IID LTT minus VERA shifted violations
paired_comparison.unit: seed cluster aggregated across four supported datasets

vera_safety.event: a deployed VERA edit violates any registered target or five-attacker leakage contract
vera_safety.success: upper bound <= 0.05 and zero observed sentinel events
vera_safety.unit: one prespecified rotating dataset decision per seed at Gamma=1.25

vera_usefulness.estimand: VERA vector safe deployments divided by exact shifted-law safe opportunities
vera_usefulness.interval: 95% percentile bootstrap over 64 seeds with 20,000 fixed resamples
vera_usefulness.success: lower confidence bound >= 0.20 at Gamma=1.1 and Gamma=1.25

real_study.primary_multiplicity: The overall confirmatory claim is made only if every primary endpoint gate passes. This is an intersection-union decision, so no alpha split is applied across the gates. Every within-rule candidate and contract correction remains as specified by the certificate.
registered_ablations: paired target harm versus edited-only target error
balanced leakage versus ordinary source accuracy
single registered attacker versus the full registered portfolio
generic scalar pooled certificate versus vector contracts
full 12-candidate frontier versus one lowest-strength candidate per eraser
one target environment versus all registered target environments
exact discrete bounds versus generic bounded-loss bounds
registered portfolio versus held-out boosted-tree stress attacker
support-aware abstention versus the prior Camelyon17 unsupported case

secondary_endpoints.per_dataset_paired_effects: two-sided exact sign tests with Holm correction over four datasets

real_study.selection_utility: Among candidates eligible for a rule, minimize maximum validation balanced leakage, then maximum environment-conditional paired target harm, then the stable candidate key.
real_study.split_policy: Group-stratify official train into 80% eraser-train and 20% construction before caps; use untouched official validation for certification; lock official external/test until all selections are complete.

statistical_tests.multiplicity: Holm correction over four externally estimable datasets
statistical_tests.primary: exact one-sided paired McNemar test, equivalent to a binomial sign test on point-only versus VERA-only external contract violations
statistical_tests.stopping_rule: run all 32 seeds; no interim testing, optional stopping, seed removal, or threshold changes
statistical_tests.unit: algorithmic seed; one paired binary outcome per supported dataset

supersedes.reason: No P0 outcome was generated under version 3. Version 4 makes the final analyzer verify its own preregistered source-file hashes before it can read P0 outcomes.
